content: The error-prone, incomplete search-based knowledge gaps in the existing literature on both curiosity-driven RL (ICM vs RND) and 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory can be framed as an unexpected opportunity: the unlearned search results that map to our collective ignorance of these domains can act as a built-in intrinsic reward signal, just like prediction errors drive curiosity in ICM/RND. This creates a self-improving cycle where agents prioritize exploring the unpopulated intellectual gaps between these two subfields, automatically directing active learning toward memory architectures that fix longstanding weaknesses in curiosity-driven exploration.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
